$doc: SINGLE SOURCE for the permission surface across all hosts. This file holds DATA only. Each host's adapter (tools/<host>/) renders it into that host's own vocabulary at setup — one data set, N labeled renderers. Never hand-edit a rendered fragment; edit here and re-render. The `renderers` block records, per host, WHETHER each surface is actually ENFORCED so the enforced-vs-advisory boundary is explicit and can never be silently crossed.

commandAllow.$doc: Only read-only commands that do not execute repository hooks or package scripts are pre-approved. Mutating git commands and every package-manager lifecycle command fall through to the host's normal confirmation policy.

secretReads.enforcement: ADVISORY — coverage is hook/sandbox-mediated, NOT pattern-enforced; do NOT imply parity

commandAllow.surface: advisory notes; command guarding is via the PreToolUse hook + sandbox
commandAllow.enforcement: advisory (notes) / enforced (hook+sandbox for the catastrophic-rm class only)

secretReads.enforcement: enforced (static deny is the reliable surface; the hook may be unreliable headless)

$contamination_guard: NEVER paste Claude `permissions.allow`/`permissions.deny` strings into Codex or Cursor config verbatim — the vocabularies differ and Codex's secret-read coverage is advisory. CI asserts rendered Codex/Cursor config never contains a literal Claude permission string.
